name: Lagoon
deck: A top-down, action RPG developed by Zoom. In it, players guide the hero, Nasir, on his quest to restore the purity of Lakeland's water.

name: Terranigma
deck: An Action-RPG from Quintet and Enix about the resurrection of the world. Ark has to fix the mess he made and travels around the globe to take part in the most important events in the history of the planet.

name: Equinox
deck: An isometric platforming game, and the sequel to Solstice. It features a series of progressively harder dungeons, as well as a number of weapons and spells that the player can use against their foes.

name: ActRaiser
deck: ActRaiser is a hybrid of two genres: city-building and action platformer. The player takes control of the powerful entity known as the Master in his quest to rebuild civilization.

name: Secret of Mana
deck: An action RPG for the Super Nintendo and the second game in the Seiken Densetsu series. Secret of Mana can be played by up to three players simultaneously, and is widely regarded as one of the system's finest games.

name: Soul Blazer
deck: An action-RPG published by Enix for the SNES. The player has to restore the world above by delving deeper into an immense dungeon.

name: Super Star Wars: Return of the Jedi
deck: An arcade-action interpretation of the third film in the original Star Wars trilogy, as Jedi Knight Luke Skywalker and his crew must rescue Han Solo and help destroy a re-built Death Star.
developers: Acclaim Studios Salt Lake City; LucasArts Entertainment Company LLC; Realtime Associates, Inc.
publishers: Black Pearl Software; JVC Musical Industries, Inc.; LucasArts Entertainment Company LLC; THQ; Victor Entertainment

name: The Blues Brothers
deck: Take control of either Jake or Elwood in this platformer.

name: Total Carnage
deck: A bloody arcade shooter developed by Midway Games, which follows the exploits of two soldiers out to kill General Akhboob.

name: Lucky Luke
deck: An action game based on the French-Belgian comic series of the same name. It was released on SNES and PC in 1997 and published and developed by Infogrames.

name: Jurassic Park
deck: An action game to coincide with the release of the 1993 movie and the first game based on the franchise. Take control of Dr. Grant and attempt to escape Jurassic Park.
developers: Limited Run Games; Ocean Software Ltd.
publishers: Jaleco Ltd.; Limited Run Games; Ocean Software Ltd.

name: Wolverine: Adamantium Rage
deck: Wolverine is on a mission to find out more about his past in this 16-bit superhero brawler from Bits Studio that covers multiple comic book arcs featuring the angriest (and Canadian-ist) member of the X-Men.
developers: Bits Studios Ltd.; Teeny Weeny Games, Ltd.

name: NBA All-Star Challenge
deck: A half-court one-on-one basketball game featuring one star player from each of the teams in the NBA. The players were chosen from the 1991-92 season NBA roster.
publishers: Acclaim Entertainment, Inc.; Flying Edge; LJN

name: Art of Fighting
deck: SNK's second foray into the fighting game genre, known for its dedicated linear story, camera zooming effect (allowing for large character sprites in close-range combat), and the introduction of a gauge that depletes when certain moves are used.
publishers: D4 Enterprise, Inc.; HAMSTER Corporation; K. Amusement Leasing Co.; SNK; TAKARA Co., Ltd.

name: Cutthroat Island
deck: An action platformer based on the 1995 movie. Female pirate captain Morgan Adams attempts to beat her villainous uncle Dawg to the treasure of Cutthroat Island.

name: Vortex
deck: An experimental shape-shifting robot is humanity's last defense in this Super FX Chip-enhanced SNES mech shooter.

name: Killer Instinct
deck: Rare's signature fighting game, featuring heavy use of pre-rendered 3D graphics, a unique match format, a special multi-sequence combo system, and C-C-C-COMBO BREAKER
publishers: Midway Games; Nintendo; Xbox Game Studios

name: Wario's Woods
deck: A tile-matching puzzle-platformer set in the Mario universe. As Toad, venture into the titular woods and use bombs to stop an invasion of monsters led by the villain Wario.

name: Kevin Keegan's Player Manager
deck: A Football Manager sim on the Super Nintendo, released in 1993. Kevin Keegan is a former England striker, and in more recent years the manager of league teams Fulham, Newcastle United and Manchester City.

name: SimCity
deck: The first installment in the highly acclaimed SimCity franchise by Maxis' Will Wright. The gameplay centers around developing and managing a city in a variety of scenarios using a combination of different zones and buildings.
developers: Intelligent Systems; Maxis Software; Nintendo EAD
publishers: Brøderbund Software, Inc.; Electronic Arts; Maxis Software; Nintendo; Superior Software

name: Pac-Man 2: The New Adventures
deck: A side-scrolling adventure game starring the eponymous arcade hero as he sets out to fetch things for his family. Instead of controlling him directly, players help guide the autonomous hero using a slingshot.

name: Populous
deck: Commonly cited as the founding title in the god game genre, Populous is a computer game from Bullfrog Productions in which the player is a deity that must help its followers become prosperous enough to overcoming rival deities and their followers.

name: Plok
deck: When the hood-headed head of the island of Akrillic gets his family heirloom flag stolen, Plok embarks on a wacky adventure in this SNES platformer.

name: The Incredible Hulk
deck: Dr. Bruce Banner's green alter-ego rampages across various levels in search of his nemesis Leader in this 16-bit side-scrolling brawler game.

name: Super Mario World
deck: One of the few launch titles of the SNES (and bundled with the system in North America and Europe), the fifth main game in the Super Mario series brings the Mario brothers into the mysterious Dinosaur Land. It marks the first appearance of Mario's dinosaur companions, known as Yoshi.

name: Stargate
deck: Stargate is loosely based on the movie of the same name. A military team with a civilian archeologist traverse across space with an ancient portal and come to blows with an alien posing as a god.
developers: Probe Entertainment; Torus Games Pty. Ltd.

name: Pushover
deck: A puzzle platformer starring an ant, his dominoes, and a connoisseur of cheesy potato puffs.
publishers: Ocean Software Ltd.; The Hit Squad

name: Kick Off 3
deck: Side-scrolling soccer game and the third in the Kick Off series. Kick Off 3 was the first game in the series to be developed by someone other than Dino Dini.

name: Micro Machines 2: Turbo Tournament
deck: Micro Machines 2: Turbo tournament is the sequel to Micro Machines. It features a top down perspective in which you race tiny cars around familiar locations such as the breakfast table or the garden.

name: Super International Cricket
deck: Hit balls for six, win by 10 wickets and use other terms confusing to Americans in the Super Nintendo's only foray into the cricket genre.

name: Warlock
deck: A young Druid warrior must find the scattered runestones through time and across the world before the Warlock does. A side-scrolling video game adaptation of the 1993 movie Warlock: The Armageddon.

name: Michael Jordan:  Chaos in the Windy City
deck: When a mad scientist uses robots and mutants to capture basketball players (for the bizarre reason of creating a cloned basketball team), it's up to then-retired professional basketball player Michael Jordan and an endless supply of basketballs to put a stop to it.

name: Last Action Hero
deck: A brawler/action game based on the 1993 Arnold Schwarzenegger film of the same name.
developers: Bits Studios Ltd.; Dome Software Developments, The; SCEE Studio Liverpool; Teeny Weeny Games, Ltd.

name: Paperboy 2
deck: Like in the original Paperboy, Paperboy 2 features a boy who delivers papers.

name: Aero the Acro-Bat
deck: Aero the Acro-bat's gameplay consists of simple platforming elements in which Aero, a red bat, that lives in a circus, must defend it from Edgar Ektor.
developers: Acclaim Studios Austin; Atomic Planet Entertainment Limited
publishers: Metro3D, Inc.; Ratalaika Games S.L.; Shinyuden; Sunsoft

name: Doom
deck: In one of the most revolutionary first-person shooters of the 1990s, players control the last remaining space marine in the demon-infested stations on Mars' moons.
developers: David A. Palmer Productions; Infinity Co., Ltd.; Nerve Software, LLC; Zenimax Media Inc; id Software
publishers: Bethesda Softworks; Softbank Corp.; id Software

name: Magic Boy
deck: Magic Boy is a platformer in which the cherubic, yet slightly creepy title character travels and captures monsters.
publishers: Empire Interactive Entertainment; JVC Digital Studios; JVC Musical Industries, Inc.

name: Kid Klown in Crazy Chase
deck: The klutziest clown Kid Klown must rescue the princess of Klown Planet from the villainous and bomb-happy Blackjack in a series of trap-filled chase levels.

name: The Hunt for Red October
deck: The Hunt for Red October is a side-scrolling submarine shooter based on the 1990 Sean Connery movie adaptation of the Tom Clancy novel.
developers: Beam Software; Krome Studios Pty Ltd.; Riedel Software Productions, Inc.

name: Angelique
deck: The first game in the popular Angelique series. It is generally acknowledged to have marked the beginning of the otome genre.

name: Super Pinball: Behind the Mask
deck: The first of two Super Pinball games released on the Super Nintendo, this game has three different pinball tables.

name: Natsuki Crisis Battle
deck: An anime licensed fighting game developed by TOSE in 1995.

name: Ultraman
deck: 1-player arcade fighter starring the tokusatsu sensation, "Ultraman".
publishers: Bandai Co., Ltd.; Banpresto Co., Ltd.

name: Mystic Ark
deck: The spiritual successor to The 7th Saga. A mute hero must find their way back to their own world.

name: Super Mahjong Taikai
deck: Released in 1992 for the Super Famicom and Turbo CD.

name: Suzuka 8 Hours
deck: An Arcade motorcycle racing game by Namco, based on the titular endurance race. A SNES version was developed by Arc System Works.
developers: Arc System Works Co., Ltd.; Namco

name: Wagyan Paradise
deck: The third Super Famicom entry in the Wagyan Land series. A pair of Wagyan siblings investigate strange events across their home island.

name: Super Dunk Star
deck: Japan-exclusive basketball video game released in 1993 for the Super Famicom. Like many non-licensed basketball games of the era, it uses fictional US teams. It also switches to dynamic shots whenever a player gets close to the net.

name: Front Mission
deck: The first video game entry in the Front Mission franchise. Wanzer forces from the USN and OCU clash over Huffman Island, with the protagonist Royd Clive caught in the middle.

name: Tetris Battle Gaiden
deck: A multiplayer focused "puzzle battle" variant on the popular Tetris, released only in Japan.

name: Fortune Quest: Dice o Korogase
deck: A Super Famicom fantasy virtual board-game that is similar to Asmik's Dokapon Kingdom series. Instead of RPG battles, the player must fight monsters via janken (rock-scissors-paper).

name: Namco Open
deck: Golf game developed by TOSE in 1993.

name: Fighter's History
deck: Nine martial artists from around the world compete in a tournament in Data East's first fighting game since the genre's Street Fighter II boom. It is best known for being the subject in a lawsuit due to its similarities with Street Fighter II.
publishers: Data East Corporation; Flying Tiger Entertainment

name: Front Mission: Gun Hazard
deck: Front Mission: Gun Hazard is a side-scrolling shooter which takes into account mech customization elements which are classic to the Front Mission series.
developers: Cobra Team; Omiya Soft, Co. Ltd.

name: Super Birdie Rush
deck: Golf video game released in 1992 exclusively for the Super Famicom. It's the sequel to Golf Club: Birdie Rush.

name: Der Langrisser
deck: A remake of Langrisser II that was originally released on the SNES in Japan and was later ported to the PC-FX.

name: Albert Odyssey
deck: A strategy RPG for the Super Famicom centered around the town of Guoto, and a war over the powerful and mysterious Key of Resurrection.

name: Syndicate
deck: An action strategy game set in a dystopian future where megacorporations control the world and wage war on each other with cybernetically-enhanced "Agents".

name: Nekketsu Tairiku: Burning Heroes
deck: A scenario-based RPG developed by J-Force and published by Enix that tells a story from the vantage point of eight different playable heroes.

name: Spindizzy Worlds
deck: This sequel to Spindizzy is an isometric, navigation-based puzzle game developed and designed by Paul Shirley.
developers: ASCII Corporation; Codemasters; Electric Dreams Software
publishers: ASCII Corporation; Activision; Arsys Software, Inc.; Codemasters; Electric Dreams Software

name: Bahamut Lagoon
deck: A tactical turn-based strategy game with battles on a grid-based map. Once two units meet in battle, the combat system follows the familiar turn-based structure.

name: Slayers
deck: A turn-based RPG for the Super Famicom based on the fantasy-comedy anime/manga series of the same name.

name: Tetris Battle Gaiden
deck: A multiplayer focused "puzzle battle" variant on the popular Tetris, released only in Japan.

name: Lagoon
deck: A top-down, action RPG developed by Zoom. In it, players guide the hero, Nasir, on his quest to restore the purity of Lakeland's water.

name: Aretha
deck: A Japanese role-playing game originally released for the Super Famicom in 1993. It follows a trilogy of Aretha RPGs for the Game Boy with a new main character and story.

name: Jimmy Connors Pro Tennis Tour
deck: A tennis game that is endorsed by American tennis champion Jimmy Connors. It is also known as "Great Courts 2" and "Pro Tennis Tour 2", prior to acquiring the Connors endorsement.
publishers: Misawa Entertainment Co.,Ltd.; The Hit Squad; Ubisoft Entertainment

name: Battle Racers
deck: Compati Hero Series racing game released in 1995 for the Super Famicom. Published by Banpresto.

name: Syndicate
deck: An action strategy game set in a dystopian future where megacorporations control the world and wage war on each other with cybernetically-enhanced "Agents".

name: The Legend of Zelda: A Link to the Past
deck: The third installment in the Zelda series makes a return to the top-down 2D gameplay of the original. Link must travel between the Light and Dark Worlds in order to set things right in the kingdom of Hyrule.

name: Terranigma
deck: An Action-RPG from Quintet and Enix about the resurrection of the world. Ark has to fix the mess he made and travels around the globe to take part in the most important events in the history of the planet.

name: ActRaiser
deck: ActRaiser is a hybrid of two genres: city-building and action platformer. The player takes control of the powerful entity known as the Master in his quest to rebuild civilization.

name: Urban Strike
deck: A millionaire, former presidential candidate, and fanatic cult leader named H. R. Malone plans on toppling the government using a super weapon he is constructing.
developers: Edge Games; Electronic Arts; Foley Hi-Tech Systems; Roklan Corporation; Solid Software

name: SimCity
deck: The first installment in the highly acclaimed SimCity franchise by Maxis' Will Wright. The gameplay centers around developing and managing a city in a variety of scenarios using a combination of different zones and buildings.
developers: Intelligent Systems; Maxis Software; Nintendo EAD
publishers: Brøderbund Software, Inc.; Electronic Arts; Maxis Software; Nintendo; Superior Software

name: Archer MacLean's Super Dropzone
deck: The sequel to the 1984 game Dropzone. As well as a graphical overhaul, the game now also features bosses and power-ups.
publishers: SCEE Studio Liverpool; UTV Ignition Entertainment

name: Addams Family Values
deck: A top-down action-adventure game based loosely on the events of the movie of the same name.

name: Killer Instinct
deck: Rare's signature fighting game, featuring heavy use of pre-rendered 3D graphics, a unique match format, a special multi-sequence combo system, and C-C-C-COMBO BREAKER
publishers: Midway Games; Nintendo; Xbox Game Studios

name: Batman Forever
deck: Foil the plans of Two-Face and The Riddler as either Batman or Robin in this brawler based on the 1995 film of the same name.

name: Arcade's Greatest Hits: The Atari Collection 1
deck: A collection of six Atari Arcade games, rereleased for consoles.
publishers: Midway Games; Williams Electronic Games, Inc.

name: Bubsy in: Claws Encounters of the Furred Kind
deck: A wise-cracking anthromorphic bobcat tries to stop enemy aliens from stealing Earth's supply of yarn balls in this 2D platformer by Accolade. What could paws-ibly go wrong?
developers: ATI Technologies, Inc.; Accolade, Inc.; Al Baker & Associates; Solid Software

name: Super Tennis
deck: Super Tennis, a 1991 tennis game for the SNES, features a Circuit mode that lets players compete to end the year at number one by accumulating ranking points on eight different tournaments.

name: Theme Park
deck: A management simulation game from Bullfrog in which the goal is to take control of an amusement park and try to make it as successful as possible.
developers: Bullfrog Productions, Ltd.; Digital Hearts Co., Ltd. ; EA Studios Japan; Krisalis Software Ltd.
publishers: Domark Software; Electronic Arts; Electronic Arts Victor; Ocean Software Ltd.

name: King Arthur's World
deck: King Arthur's World is a side-scrolling strategy game where the player must utilise various units and their abilities to help King Arthur safely reach the end of each level.

name: Stunt Race FX
deck: A SNES-exclusive racing game that utilizes the Super FX chip to bring stylized vehicles to life in polygonal 3D.

name: Doom
deck: In one of the most revolutionary first-person shooters of the 1990s, players control the last remaining space marine in the demon-infested stations on Mars' moons.
developers: David A. Palmer Productions; Infinity Co., Ltd.; Nerve Software, LLC; Zenimax Media Inc; id Software
publishers: Bethesda Softworks; Softbank Corp.; id Software

name: Super Star Wars: Return of the Jedi
deck: An arcade-action interpretation of the third film in the original Star Wars trilogy, as Jedi Knight Luke Skywalker and his crew must rescue Han Solo and help destroy a re-built Death Star.
developers: Acclaim Studios Salt Lake City; LucasArts Entertainment Company LLC; Realtime Associates, Inc.
publishers: Black Pearl Software; JVC Musical Industries, Inc.; LucasArts Entertainment Company LLC; THQ; Victor Entertainment

name: Super Punch-Out!!
deck: The second home adaptation of Nintendo's Punch-Out!! series of boxing arcade games, pitting players against sixteen pugilists from around the world (each with their own variety of techniques, a lot of which are illegal).

name: Street Fighter Alpha 2
deck: The sequel to (and remake of) Street Fighter Alpha brings five new fighters into the fray including newcomer schoolgirl Sakura while adding a new special ability: the "Custom Combo", in which players can trigger a massive timed combo chain using their Super Meter.

name: Jurassic Park
deck: An action game to coincide with the release of the 1993 movie and the first game based on the franchise. Take control of Dr. Grant and attempt to escape Jurassic Park.
developers: Limited Run Games; Ocean Software Ltd.
publishers: Jaleco Ltd.; Limited Run Games; Ocean Software Ltd.

name: Cliffhanger
deck: A side-scrolling brawler with a number of precarious action segments, loosely based on the Sylvester Stallone action film of the same name.

name: World Cup USA '94
deck: A soccer game based on the 1994 FIFA World Cup tournament hosted by the USA.

name: Disney's Timon & Pumbaa's Jungle Games
deck: A mini-game collection featuring Timon and Pumbaa from Disney's The Lion King aimed at a younger audience. Released for PC and Mac and later ported to the Super Nintendo Entertainment System.

name: American Bass Challenge
deck: A challenging simulation take on virtual fishing

name: Lemmings 2: The Tribes
deck: Navigate the fragile little fellas through even trickier levels. The addition of several new lemming "classes" is this game's main difference from its predecessor.

name: Super Battletank 2
deck: A first-person tank simulator and the third game in Garry Kitchen's Battletank franchise.

name: Walt Disney's The Jungle Book
deck: A 2D platformer based on the 1967 animated Disney movie and released on multiple platforms. It was one of four Virgin Interactive platformers based on Disney movies, along with The Lion King, Aladdin and Pinocchio.
developers: Eurocom Entertainment Software; Pencil Test Studios; Virgin Interactive Entertainment, Inc.

name: Tetris & Dr. Mario
deck: Two of the most popular Nintendo puzzle games, now together on the SNES!

name: Jimmy Connors Pro Tennis Tour
deck: A tennis game that is endorsed by American tennis champion Jimmy Connors. It is also known as "Great Courts 2" and "Pro Tennis Tour 2", prior to acquiring the Connors endorsement.
publishers: Misawa Entertainment Co.,Ltd.; The Hit Squad; Ubisoft Entertainment

name: Equinox
deck: An isometric platforming game, and the sequel to Solstice. It features a series of progressively harder dungeons, as well as a number of weapons and spells that the player can use against their foes.

name: Kirby's Dream Course
deck: Kirby's Dream Course is a mini-golf simulator with a unique Kirby twist. It was released by HAL Laboratory for the SNES and later became available in the Wii Shop's Virtual Console.

name: WWF Super Wrestlemania
deck: A wrestling game based on WWF (now WWE) and the first of the LJN-trilogy of WWF games in the 16-bit era.
developers: Acclaim Entertainment, Inc.; Acclaim Studios Salt Lake City
publishers: Acclaim Entertainment, Inc.; Flying Edge; LJN

name: Lucky Luke
deck: An action game based on the French-Belgian comic series of the same name. It was released on SNES and PC in 1997 and published and developed by Infogrames.

name: Pilotwings
deck: A first-party flight simulation game for SNES featuring a variety of aerial vehicles and challenges.

name: Eek! the Cat
deck: A 1994 SNES puzzle-platformer based on Savage Steve Holland's animated series about a selfless and luckless purple cat. Eek must protect several companions from harm, usually putting himself in danger in the process.

name: Stargate
deck: Stargate is loosely based on the movie of the same name. A military team with a civilian archeologist traverse across space with an ancient portal and come to blows with an alien posing as a god.
developers: Probe Entertainment; Torus Games Pty. Ltd.

name: Donkey Kong Country 2: Diddy's Kong Quest
deck: When Donkey Kong is kong-napped by the evil Kaptain K. Rool, it's up to nephew Diddy (and his girlfriend Dixie) to traverse Crocodile Isle to rescue him!

name: Disney's Beauty and the Beast
deck: The official licensed game of the 1991 movie of the same name.
publishers: Hudson Entertainment, Inc.; Virgin Interactive Entertainment, Inc.

name: Super Bomberman
deck: Super Bomberman was the first Bomberman game to appear on the SNES, and the first game on that system to feature 4 player simultaneous play via the multitap.

name: Disney's Aladdin
deck: Disney's Aladdin was the title of several different games from different developers based on the classic Disney film, Aladdin. As the titular character, players venture through many levels inspired by events and locations in the movie.
developers: Capcom; NMS Software Ltd.; SIMS; Virgin Interactive Entertainment, Inc.
publishers: Capcom; Sega; Virgin Interactive Entertainment, Inc.; iam8bit

name: F1 Pole Position 2
deck: A Formula One racing game released only in Europe and Japan for the Super Nintendo. Known in Japan as "Human Grand Prix II", and is the second of four SNES games in the series.

name: Lagoon
deck: A top-down, action RPG developed by Zoom. In it, players guide the hero, Nasir, on his quest to restore the purity of Lakeland's water.

name: Super Ghouls 'N Ghosts
deck: The third game in the classic Ghosts 'n Goblins series sees the valiant knight Arthur once again embarking on a brutally difficult quest to rescue his beloved from demonic forces.

name: Solid Runner
deck: After his fiancee is murdered, Ion City detective Shuu takes on the city's underworld to get revenge in his customized mech.

name: First Samurai
deck: A 2D action-platforming game that begins in a mystical representation of feudal Japan but travels all over time. It was originally released on European home computers, though the 1993 SNES port by Kemco was published in all regions.

name: Ashita no Joe
deck: A boxing game based on a critically-acclaimed dramatic manga. It is the follow-up to Legend of Success Joe for the Neo Geo.

name: Light Fantasy
deck: Light Fantasy is a Japan-exclusive RPG.

name: SimCity
deck: The fifth major installment of the SimCity franchise is a new take on the old city simulation formula. It features asynchronous multiplayer as well as Maxis' new Glassbox engine, allowing for real time customization and upgrading of buildings.

name: NBA Live 97
deck: NBA Live '97 featured Mitch Richmond of the Sacramento Kings on the cover, the first in the series to dedicate the cover to a single player.

name: FIFA 98: Road to World Cup
deck: FIFA 98: Road to World Cup is a multiplatform instalment of the popular soccer video game series, allowing players to relive moments from the 1998 FIFA World Cup.

name: NHLPA Hockey '93
deck: This is the 2nd EA NHL game, although it lacked the NHL license and only had all the player names and city names appearing.

name: NHL 95
deck: The 4th game in the NHL hockey franchise from EA Sports. It was released for the 1994-1995 NHL season.
developers: EA Sports; High Score Productions; Visual Concepts

name: Young Merlin
deck: A top-down action-adventure SNES game in which Merlin, as a young man, must save a fantasy kingdom from the Shadow King.

name: Skyblazer
deck: Skyblazer is an action platformer from Sony Imagesoft, released exclusively on the Super Nintendo.
publishers: 989 Studios; Sony Interactive Entertainment America; Sony Music Entertainment (Japan), Inc.

name: Rise of the Robots
deck: An infamous sci-fi fighting game known for its expensive marketing campaign (failing to live up to such lofty expectations). Set in the far future 2043, players control a cyborg that must neutralize an army of robots infected with a powerful computer virus.
developers: Data Design Interactive; Instinct Design; Mirage Technologies (Multimedia) Ltd.
publishers: Absolute Entertainment; Acclaim Entertainment, Inc.; Philips Interactive Media, Inc.; T&E Soft, Inc.; Time Warner Interactive, Inc.

name: Mighty Max
deck: A 16-bit action platformer game based on the TV show of the same name which in turn was based on the toyline of the same name. Max's friends Bea and Felix are also playable characters.
publishers: Ocean Software Ltd.; Sony Electronic Publishing Ltd.

name: Tetris Attack
deck: One of the progenitors of the "swap and match" tile-based action-puzzle games, featuring either a Yoshi theme (for its international "Tetris Attack" release) or a cutesy fairy theme (for its Japanese "Panel de Pon" release).

name: Tiny Toon Adventures: Buster Busts Loose!
deck: Take the role of Buster Bunny from the animated series "Tiny Toons" in this crazy SNES action-platformer.

name: Indiana Jones' Greatest Adventures
deck: A Super Nintendo game that recreates the classic Indiana Jones film trilogy. Players follow Indiana's steps from Raiders of the Lost Ark up until The Last Crusade in this platformer using Indy's famous whip as well as various guns and grenades.
developers: Factor 5, LLC; LucasArts Entertainment Company LLC
publishers: JVC Musical Industries, Inc.; LucasArts Entertainment Company LLC; Victor Entertainment

name: Putty
deck: Whoever thought that a ball of putty could be so tough?
publishers: System 3 Software, Ltd.; U.S. Gold Ltd.; Varie

name: Accele Brid
deck: Accele Brid is a behind-the-back shooter starring three large robots, released only in Japan for the SNES in 1993.

name: Super Mario World 2: Yoshi's Island
deck: A spin-off of the Super Mario platforming series, Yoshi's Island puts players in control of a tribe of Yoshies as they guide the infant Mario past the forces of the evil Kamek.

name: International Superstar Soccer
deck: The first in the franchise, ISS featured a realistic simulation style soccer game played on a skewed almost isometric grid.
developers: Konami Computer Entertainment Osaka Co., Ltd.

name: Soul Blazer
deck: An action-RPG published by Enix for the SNES. The player has to restore the world above by delving deeper into an immense dungeon.

name: Mega Man X
deck: The spiritual successor to the original Mega Man series of platformers, Mega Man X features a more technologically advanced hero and a darker storyline (set a century after the adventures of the original Mega Man).

name: Super Castlevania IV
deck: An enhanced recreation of the original Castlevania, retelling Simon Belmont's crusade against Dracula with new levels, content, and gameplay elements.

name: Super Street Fighter II
deck: Four new warriors enter the World Warrior tournament in the fourth main iteration of the classic Street Fighter II series. Along with the roster change, the game received numerous graphical and audio upgrades thanks to its more powerful CPS-2 hardware.
developers: Capcom; Freestyle; Rozner Labs Software Group

name: Secret of Mana
deck: An action RPG for the Super Nintendo and the second game in the Seiken Densetsu series. Secret of Mana can be played by up to three players simultaneously, and is widely regarded as one of the system's finest games.

name: Pocky & Rocky
deck: Pocky and Rocky is an arcade style top-down style shooting game starring a girl and a raccoon.

name: Ardy Lightfoot
deck: A mascot platformer that was developed by ASCII, published my Titus and was released in 1993 in Japan and 1994 for Europe and North America.

name: Tintin in Tibet
deck: Tintin au Tibet is a platformer based on the comic of the same name.

name: Super Dunk Star
deck: Japan-exclusive basketball video game released in 1993 for the Super Famicom. Like many non-licensed basketball games of the era, it uses fictional US teams. It also switches to dynamic shots whenever a player gets close to the net.

name: Zico Soccer
deck: Japan-exclusive soccer management game released in 1994 by Electronic Arts Victor. Endorsed by Zico, a famous Brazilian soccer player.

name: Rise of the Robots
deck: An infamous sci-fi fighting game known for its expensive marketing campaign (failing to live up to such lofty expectations). Set in the far future 2043, players control a cyborg that must neutralize an army of robots infected with a powerful computer virus.
developers: Data Design Interactive; Instinct Design; Mirage Technologies (Multimedia) Ltd.
publishers: Absolute Entertainment; Acclaim Entertainment, Inc.; Philips Interactive Media, Inc.; T&E Soft, Inc.; Time Warner Interactive, Inc.

name: Stealth
deck: A turn-based military tactics game in which the player controls a squad of soldiers moving through enemy territory. It was released on the Super Famicom in Japan only.

name: Fortune Quest: Dice o Korogase
deck: A Super Famicom fantasy virtual board-game that is similar to Asmik's Dokapon Kingdom series. Instead of RPG battles, the player must fight monsters via janken (rock-scissors-paper).

name: Casper
deck: A licensed game based on the 1995 movie featuring Christina Ricci and Bill Pullman. Casper and his human friend Kat work together to foil the plans of an evil rich heiress and her assistant.
developers: Absolute Entertainment; Funcom Productions A/S; G3 Interactive Ltd.; Natsume

name: Ghoul Patrol
deck: The 1994 sequel to LucasArts' Zombies Ate My Neighbors. Join Zeke and Julie once again as another horrible evil is unleashed upon their town.
publishers: JVC Musical Industries, Inc.; Victor Entertainment

name: Battle Submarine
deck: A real-time tactical submarine combat simulator for the Super Famicom.

name: Joushou Mahjong Tenpai
deck: A riichi mahjong game for the Super Famicom, including multiple training tools.

name: Gekisou Sentai Carranger: Zenkai! Racer Senshi!
deck: Cartoony action/platformer starring Carranger (Turbo Rangers). Part of Bandai's range of compact "Sufami Turbo" series.

name: Super Bomberman
deck: Super Bomberman was the first Bomberman game to appear on the SNES, and the first game on that system to feature 4 player simultaneous play via the multitap.

name: Front Mission
deck: The first video game entry in the Front Mission franchise. Wanzer forces from the USN and OCU clash over Huffman Island, with the protagonist Royd Clive caught in the middle.

name: Nekketsu Tairiku: Burning Heroes
deck: A scenario-based RPG developed by J-Force and published by Enix that tells a story from the vantage point of eight different playable heroes.

name: Battle Pinball
deck: A pinball Compati Hero Series game released in 1995 for the Super Famicom.

name: Dekitate High School
deck: A combination school building sim and schoolgirl mentoring sim for the Super Famicom in 1995.

name: Bahamut Lagoon
deck: A tactical turn-based strategy game with battles on a grid-based map. Once two units meet in battle, the combat system follows the familiar turn-based structure.

name: Natsuki Crisis Battle
deck: An anime licensed fighting game developed by TOSE in 1995.

name: Der Langrisser
deck: A remake of Langrisser II that was originally released on the SNES in Japan and was later ported to the PC-FX.

name: True Lies
deck: A top-down shooter based on the 1994 action film of the same name starring Arnold Schwarzenegger where he is trying to get to the bottom of a terrorist organization.

name: Emerald Dragon
deck: Emerald Dragon is a traditional Japanese RPG released for various gaming consoles and computers.
publishers: Glodia; Media Works Inc.; NEC Home Electronics, Ltd.

name: Super Trump Collection
deck: A compilation of seven card games for the Super Famicom.

name: Marmalade Boy
deck: Dating game developed by Kuusou Kagaku in 1995.

name: Aretha
deck: A Japanese role-playing game originally released for the Super Famicom in 1993. It follows a trilogy of Aretha RPGs for the Game Boy with a new main character and story.

name: Albert Odyssey
deck: A strategy RPG for the Super Famicom centered around the town of Guoto, and a war over the powerful and mysterious Key of Resurrection.
